region_of_origin: United Kingdom (Schweppes brand; product globally distributed)
food_subgroup: Carbonated non-alcoholic beverages — plain soda water

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301638 represents carbonated water as a category; no brand-specific Schweppes entry exists in FoodOn. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Water (including mineral water and other bottled water)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers water broadly; no specific carbonated soda water sub-code identified. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 is the closest defensible anchor for water-based beverages; no brand-specific soda water code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can or bottle of Schweppes Soda Water is approximately 330 ml (330 g), the typical serving size sold in Singapore and convenience outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated water, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14412)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Schweppes Soda Water is plain carbonated water with no added sugars, flavours, or minerals beyond trace CO2-derived carbonic acid. USDA FoodData Central entry for plain carbonated water (soda water) is an exact compositional match. Values confirmed against USDA Branded Food entry for Schweppes Soda Water (FDC ID 1104477) which reports 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate per 240 ml serving.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — water and carbonated water reference; University of Sydney GI Database convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain soda water contains no glycemic carbohydrates (0 g carbohydrate per 100 g confirmed by USDA). The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a formal GI to zero-carbohydrate beverages. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) to indicate effectively zero glycemic impact. gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (~5 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Schweppes Soda Water contains approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 ml, which is nutritionally insignificant and poses no meaningful sodium load for individuals managing hypertension; it is a suitable hydration choice.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Schweppes Soda Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic index effectively equal to zero, making it a safe and appropriate beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes who require hydration without glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, zero calories
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Schweppes Soda Water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no calories, and therefore does not contribute to cardiovascular risk factors; it is a heart-neutral hydration option suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Schweppes Soda Water is a plain carbonated water beverage produced by the Schweppes brand, one of the world's oldest and most widely distributed soft drink manufacturers. It consists of purified water infused with carbon dioxide (CO2) under pressure, producing characteristic effervescence and a mildly crisp, slightly acidic taste due to the formation of carbonic acid. It contains no added sugars, flavourings, artificial sweeteners, or significant minerals. Nutritionally, it is essentially calorie-free, with zero protein, zero fat, zero carbohydrate, and negligible sodium (~5 mg per 100 ml). It is suitable for all major dietary patterns including vegan, vegetarian, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. In Singapore, soda water is commonly served as a mixer with alcoholic beverages, as a standalone refreshment, or as a palate cleanser in Middle Eastern, Western, and international restaurant settings. Clinically, it is safe for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, as it contributes no glycemic load, no sodium burden, and no lipid intake. The glycemic index is effectively zero; by convention it is recorded as 1 (low category) since no carbohydrate is present to elicit a glycemic response.
common_user_queries: Is Schweppes Soda Water calorie free?
Is soda water good for high blood pressure?
What is the difference between soda water and sparkling water?
How many calories in a can of Schweppes Soda Water?
Is soda water the same as club soda?
Can I drink soda water on a diet?
Is carbonated water bad for you?

confidence_reason: Schweppes Soda Water is a well-characterised commercial product with a simple, consistent formulation (water + CO2). Nutrient values are confirmed by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain carbonated water and cross-validated against the USDA Branded Food entry for Schweppes Soda Water. All core macros are confirmed zero by formulation and approved source reporting.
notes: Zero-macro justification: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a plain carbonated water with no caloric ingredients. protein_g contains no protein — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. fat_g contains no fat — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. carbohydrate_g contains no carbohydrate — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. sugar_g is naturally zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre. sodium_mg is approximately 5 mg per 100 ml per USDA SR Legacy data for plain carbonated water; this trace amount reflects natural mineral content of the water source. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not consistently reported across sources for this product. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for plain carbonated water. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Cuisine tag in input was 'Middle Eastern' reflecting the field survey restaurant context (Sahara Bar & Restaurant); the product itself is a globally distributed brand beverage and has been classified accordingly. Multilingual names provided following Singaporean dish rules given the Singapore field survey context, covering English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. Aliases consistency check (iteration 3): all strings present in aliases_by_language verified verbatim in flat aliases array — english entries (9), chinese entries (2: 苏打水, 蘇打水), malay entries (1: Air Soda), tamil entries (2: சோடா நீர், சோடா தண்ணீர்), hindi entries (2: सोडा वाटर, सोडा पानी), romanized entries (1: Soda Shui) — all 17 unique strings confirmed present in flat aliases list. Previous iteration q8 failure was caused by mixed-script hindi entry 'சோடா वाटर' and 'சோடா पानी' in aliases_by_language not matching flat aliases entries; corrected in this iteration by using pure Devanagari script for both hindi aliases (सोडा वाटर, सोडा पानी) consistently across both aliases_by_language and flat aliases.

q1_food_identity.notes: canonical English name present and multilingual identity satisfied

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: core macros and nutrient source integrity pass

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: secondary nutrient fields are present and numeric

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: GI, GI category, GL, and GI source are complete

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: ontology anchor(s) present: foodon_uri, foodex2_code, snomed_ct_code

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: alias coverage is adequate and multilingual alias consistency passes

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Applied zero-macro justification rule, zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, sentinel values documentation rule, ontology match_type rule, and multilingual naming rules for Singapore context.
Iteration 2: Added 'Soda Shui' to flat aliases to resolve q8 failure from iteration 1.

Iteration 3: Identified root cause of persistent q8 failure — mixed-script hindi aliases ('சோடா वाटर', 'சோடா पानी') in aliases_by_language used Tamil script characters mixed with Devanagari, causing verbatim mismatch with flat aliases. Corrected by using pure Devanagari script (सोडा वाटर, सोडा पानी) consistently in both aliases_by_language.hindi and flat aliases array. Performed full cross-check of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases to confirm complete consistency.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
